+# AI Dispatch Log
+
+- Timestamp: 2026-05-26T02:41:41.5511410+03:00
+- Dispatch: $Id
+- Issue: #198 - Add snapshot-backed mutation retries
+- Issue URL: https://github.com/RustCADs/RGE/issues/198
+- Branch: $Branch
+- Loop exit code: $LoopExit
+- Codex control verdict: $Verdict
+- Loop log: $LoopLog
+
+## Process Trace
+
+1. Queue selected the oldest open ai-dispatch issue.
+2. Queue labelled the issue ai-dispatch-running.
+3. Queue created branch ai-dispatch/ISSUE-198.
+4. Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 ran Codex plan, Claude gate, Claude execute, and Codex control.
+5. Queue wrote this detailed log before staging, committing, merging, or pushing.
+6. If and only if exit code is 0 and Codex control verdict is pass, queue will fast-forward main and push origin/main.
+
+## Files Changed / Added / Deleted
+
+git status --short --untracked-files=all before the queue commit:
+
+~~~text

+?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_EXEC_2026-05-26_02-34-43+0300.md

+?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.md
+?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.meta.json
+~~~
+
+git diff --name-status before the queue commit:
+
+~~~text

+~~~
+
+git diff --stat before the queue commit:
+
+~~~text
+Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 | 223 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++--
+ 1 file changed, 217 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)
+~~~
+
+This log file is also added by the queue before publish:
+
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_2026-05-26_02-41-41+0300.md
+
+## Generated Run Files
+

+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-198/verification.round0.log (1081558 bytes)
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-198/codex.prompt.md (1050 bytes)
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-198/codex.control.round0.json (3578 bytes)

+
+## Codex Control JSON

+{"verdict":"pass","summary":"Implementation is ready for queue commit/publish. The diff is scoped to Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 plus the ISSUE-198 handoff artifacts, adds bounded MutationRetryCount support, preserves semantic verdict handling outside mutation retry, and restores phase-entry tracked plus untracked non-ignored state before retry attempts. Canonical verification passed per .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-198/verification.round0.log.","task_packet":"ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.md","exec_packet":"ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_EXEC_2026-05-26_02-34-43+0300.md","changed_files":["Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1","ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.md","ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.meta.json","ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_EXEC_2026-05-26_02-34-43+0300.md","ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_EXEC_2026-05-26_02-34-43+0300.meta.json"],"required_fixes":[],"verification":[{"command":"git status --short --branch","result":"On branch ai-dispatch/ISSUE-198. EXEC report claims are substantively corroborated by the diff and verification log; actual final status additionally includes the generated EXEC packet and sidecar, which are allowed artifacts.","exit_code":0}],"commit_readiness":"ready_for_publish","commands_run":["git status --short --branch","git diff --stat","git diff","Get-Content -LiteralPath 'ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.md'","Get-Content -LiteralPath 'ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_EXEC_2026-05-26_02-34-43+0300.md'","Get-Content -LiteralPath '.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-198/verification.round0.log'","Get-Content -LiteralPath 'Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1' | Select-Object -First 260","Get-Content -LiteralPath 'Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1' | Select-Object -Skip 220 -First 160","Get-Content -LiteralPath 'Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1' | Select-Object -Skip 420 -First 190","Get-Content -LiteralPath 'Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1' | Select-Object -Skip 980 -First 100","Get-Content -LiteralPath 'Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1' | Select-Object -Skip 1240 -First 340","Get-Content -LiteralPath 'Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1' | Select-Object -Skip 1540 -First 60","git diff --check","git status --short --untracked-files=all","git diff --cached --stat","Select-String -Path '.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-198/verification.round0.log' -Pattern '^==='","Get-Content -LiteralPath 'AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md' | Select-Object -First 220","Get-Content -LiteralPath 'ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md' | Select-Object -First 220"]}

@@ -0,0 +1,176 @@
+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-198
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_02-34-43+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 — added MutationRetryCount parameter, snapshot/restore helpers, mutation-retry wrapper, and three call-site integrations
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`:
+  - Added `MutationRetryCount` parameter, `[ValidateRange(0, 5)]`, default
+    `1`. Value `0` is the previous single-attempt path: no snapshot, no
+    restore, no retry output.
+  - Added `New-MutationSnapshot` / `Restore-MutationSnapshot` /
+    `Remove-MutationSnapshot` helpers immediately after
+    `Invoke-WithSamePhaseRetry`. Tracked changes (staged + unstaged) ride
+    on a pinned `git stash create` commit under
+    `refs/dispatch-snapshot/<safe-label>`. Untracked non-ignored files are
+    captured into an in-memory `[ordered]` byte map via
+    `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard`.
+  - Added `Invoke-WithMutationRetry`: when `RetryCount > 0`, it captures
+    one phase-entry snapshot, then for each attempt failure runs
+    `git reset --hard HEAD && git clean -fd`, replays the stash with
+    `git stash apply --index`, and rewrites the captured untracked
+    manifest. Ignored paths (`.ai/dispatch-*/`, `*.log`, `/target/`) are
+    deliberately left alone, so external caches and run-dir transcripts
+    persist across retries. On exhaustion the wrapper clears
+    `RetryableFailEnabled` and re-enters the canonical `Fail` path so the
+    queue runner / scheduled task still sees the original terminal wording
+    and exit behavior.
+  - Wired the wrapper around three call sites:
+    1. The `Invoke-ClaudeExecute` round-loop call — the `EXEC_STATUS`
+       semantic check (`blocked` / `failed`) stays OUTSIDE the wrapper, so
+       those verdicts are still terminal and never retried.
+    2. The verification-failure `Invoke-CorrectionPacket` call — both the
+       correction-packet write and the `Assert-PlannerScopeClean` guard
+       run inside the retried action so an out-of-scope correction is
+       reverted before the next attempt.
+    3. The control-`needs_changes` `Invoke-CorrectionPacket` call —
+       identical wrapping, identical scope-clean guarantee.
+  - Side effect: normalized `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` output (which
+    uses forward slashes on Windows) before composing untracked-manifest
+    paths with `Join-Path`.
+
+The existing same-phase retry around the two read-only model review phases
+(`Invoke-ClaudePlanGate`, `Invoke-CodexControl`) is untouched. Plan-gate
+and control `needs_changes` / `block` verdicts keep their current
+behavior; only invocation / contract failures are retried. The new
+parameter is range-validated identically to the existing retry knobs.
+
+### Tests
+
+- No permanent test files added (TASK forbids `New tests, harness
+  scripts...`). The focused restore harness is a one-shot PowerShell
+  script under `%TEMP%` that loads the helpers from
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` via AST, stubs `Fail`, runs four assertions
+  against a throwaway `git init` workspace, and tears the workspace down
+  in `finally`. The harness file itself was removed after the run.
+
+### Docs
+
+- No doc changes (TASK forbids new docs / ADRs / templates).
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Single-file change. The retry wrapper short-circuits on `RetryCount=0`,
+returning the action's value with no snapshot or output, so callers in
+the zero-retry path behave exactly as before. With `RetryCount > 0` the
+phase-entry snapshot is captured once and reused across attempts.
+Snapshot restoration is git-driven (`reset --hard`, `clean -fd`,
+`stash apply --index`) plus an in-memory rewrite of untracked
+non-ignored files; nothing outside the repository root is ever touched.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- PowerShell parser validation
+  `[System.Management.Automation.PSParser]::Tokenize` over
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` → 0 errors, exit 0.
+- `git diff --check` (from repo root) → exit 0 (no whitespace errors).
+- Focused restore harness
+  `%TEMP%\rge-issue198-harness-5573b70f\harness.ps1` against a
+  throwaway `git init` repo:
+  - T1 snapshot/restore mechanics — phase-entry state had a tracked
+    unstaged modification, a tracked staged modification, a tracked
+    deletion, an untracked non-ignored file, and an ignored file. Failed
+    attempt corrupted the unstaged tracked file further, restaged the
+    tracked file with corrupted content, removed the untracked, dropped
+    a stray untracked, and resurrected the deleted file with the wrong
+    content and a staged add. After `Restore-MutationSnapshot` the
+    worktree, index, and untracked set matched the phase-entry exactly;
+    the ignored file remained; the stray untracked was removed; the
+    snapshot ref was deleted by `Remove-MutationSnapshot`. PASS.
+  - T2 `Invoke-WithMutationRetry -RetryCount 0` — action ran exactly
+    once, no `[mutation-retry]` text emitted, the dirty worktree and
+    stray untracked left by the failing action were NOT restored
+    (single-attempt path preserved). PASS.
+  - T3 `RetryCount=1`, fail-then-succeed — attempt 1 corrupted a
+    phase-entry untracked file and created a stray, then `Fail`'d;
+    wrapper emitted the four required lines (`attempt 1 failed`,
+    `restoring worktree`, `worktree restored`, `succeeded on attempt 2`);
+    attempt 2 observed the restored phase-entry state and returned the
+    expected value, which the wrapper propagated. PASS.
+  - T4 `RetryCount=1`, fail-both — exactly 2 attempts ran, snapshot
+    restore ran between them, exhaustion message emitted, the original
+    failure path (via `Fail`) surfaced the final attempt's message.
+    PASS.
+  - Harness exit code 0; temp dir removed.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` (canonical CI gate) → `VERIFY OK: all 7
+  verification step(s) passed.` Steps: cargo +nightly fmt --check,
+  architecture lints (all enforcement + supplementary PASS), the
+  arch-lint test suite, cargo build, cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast,
+  cargo test --workspace --doc, cargo bench --no-run. Exit 0.
+- Final `git status --short --untracked-files=all`:
+  ```
+   M Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.md
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.meta.json
+  ```
+  Only the allowed `MAY edit` file is modified; the two untracked entries
+  are the input TASK packet and its sidecar (untouched per scope).
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope.
+
+Note on snapshot implementation choice: the TASK calls for tracked +
+untracked-non-ignored preservation. A first cut used
+`git stash create -u` and `git stash apply --index` for both. A
+focused-harness probe revealed that Git for Windows' `git stash apply`
+does NOT extract the untracked parent tree (exit 0, no error, untracked
+files silently missing). The fix is split capture: tracked changes ride
+on `git stash create` (which `git stash apply --index` does restore
+correctly), and untracked non-ignored files are spooled to an in-memory
+byte map. This still preserves "the exact phase-entry tracked state plus
+the exact phase-entry set and contents of untracked non-ignored files"
+required by the TASK, but does it via a path that actually works on this
+platform. No semantic dispatch outcome was reclassified as retryable.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+None. The wrapper deliberately keeps semantic verdicts (`EXEC_STATUS:
+blocked|failed`, control `needs_changes|block`, plan-gate
+`needs_changes|block`, verification failures) outside the retry envelope,
+so the existing correction-round / max-revision / queue-publish behavior
+is unchanged.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` (modified, uncommitted).
+- Untracked items: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.meta.json` (the
+  active TASK packet and its sidecar; pre-existing dispatch artifacts).
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-198`
+- Last commit: `75ac291` chore(dispatch): queue mutation retry task
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-198
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

+    "related_files": [
+                          "Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 — added MutationRetryCount parameter, snapshot/restore helpers, mutation-retry wrapper, and three call-site integrations"

@@ -0,0 +1,129 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-198
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_EXEC_*.meta.json
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-198*.md
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Implement GitHub issue #198 by adding snapshot-backed same-phase retry support for the two dispatch-loop mutation phases that can edit the repository: Claude execution and Codex correction-packet creation. The retry must protect pre-existing and in-progress work by restoring the exact phase-entry tracked state plus untracked non-ignored files before each retry attempt, while leaving all semantic dispatch outcomes and queue/publish behavior unchanged.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+- This dispatch's own generated EXEC packet matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_EXEC_*.md`
+- This dispatch's own generated EXEC sidecar matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_EXEC_*.meta.json`
+- This dispatch's own `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-198*.md` artifact if the queue runner or focused harness produces one
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1
+- Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1
+- Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1
+- Wait-GitHubActions.ps1
+- Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1
+- .ai/dispatch.verify.ps1
+- .ai/codex_control.schema.json
+- .ai/dispatch.tasks.md
+- ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md
+- ai_handoffs/templates/
+- Cargo.toml
+- Cargo.lock
+- Any Rust source, tests, docs, ADRs, CI workflows, scheduler files, schema files, JSONL schema or trace files, failure-taxonomy files, GitHub issue or label automation, or task brief/backlog content
+- Any packet for a dispatch other than ISSUE-198
+- This TASK packet after execution begins
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one EXEC packet for this dispatch under `ai_handoffs/`
+- The matching `.meta.json` sidecar for that EXEC packet if packet finalization is allowed by the active packet
+- A dispatch-owned `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-198*.md` artifact only if produced by the queue runner or by documenting the focused restore harness
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New tests, harness scripts, docs, ADRs, schemas, JSONL files, Cargo files, Rust files, CI files, scheduler files, queue/auto artifacts, GitHub automation files, or protocol templates
+- New packets other than the single allowed ISSUE-198 EXEC packet
+- Persistent harness files in the repository
+- Any file outside `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`, this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/` artifacts, and this dispatch's own `ai_dispatch_logs/` artifacts
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- Add a mutation retry control path in `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` that applies only to the phases invoking `Invoke-ClaudeExecute` and `Invoke-CorrectionPacket`.
+- Add a user-visible zero-retry path, such as a bounded `MutationRetryCount` parameter with `0` supported, where mutation behavior remains the previous single-attempt behavior: no retry, no restore attempt after failure, and no extra retry success or exhaustion output.
+- Capture a phase-entry snapshot before each retry-enabled mutation phase. The snapshot must preserve tracked file state, including staged and unstaged tracked changes if present, plus untracked non-ignored files and their contents.
+- Restore the captured phase-entry state before every mutation retry attempt. Restoration must remove files created by the failed attempt, undo tracked edits/deletions from the failed attempt, and recreate any phase-entry untracked non-ignored files.
+- Wire snapshot-backed retry around Claude execution rounds only at the `Invoke-ClaudeExecute` call site. Do not include verification, EXEC_STATUS semantic handling, or Codex control in that retry scope.
+- Wire snapshot-backed retry around correction-packet creation at both existing correction call sites. Include the existing planner scope-clean check in the retried mutation phase so a failed or out-of-scope correction attempt is restored before retrying.
+- Emit human-readable loop output for mutation retry failure, worktree restore before retry, retry success, and retry exhaustion. Use ordinary loop text output only; do not add or change schemas, JSONL trace formats, or structured log contracts.
+- Preserve the existing read-only same-phase retry behavior for Claude plan gate and Codex control. Do not convert semantic verdicts into retryable errors.
+- Produce the ISSUE-198 EXEC packet with a concise implementation summary, restore-harness result, verification results, and final git status.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- `Invoke-ClaudeExecute` receives snapshot-backed retry for retryable invocation/contract failures only. A returned `EXEC_STATUS: blocked` or `EXEC_STATUS: failed` remains terminal for that round and is not retried.
+- `Invoke-CorrectionPacket` receives snapshot-backed retry for retryable mutation failures, including failures caught by the planner scope-clean guard after the correction packet writer returns.
+- Verification failure remains a correction-round input or terminal failure according to existing `MaxCorrectionRounds` behavior; it is not retried as a same-phase mutation failure.
+- Codex control `needs_changes` and `block` verdicts keep their current behavior and are not retried as failures.
+- Claude plan-gate `needs_changes` and `block` verdicts keep their current behavior and are not retried as failures.
+- `MutationRetryCount` or the chosen equivalent is range-limited, defaults to enabling one mutation retry, and supports `0` as a previous-behavior single-attempt path.
+- With zero mutation retries configured, a failed Claude execution invocation or failed correction-packet mutation takes the same single-attempt failure path as before this dispatch, aside from unavoidable code organization changes.
+- Before each mutation retry attempt, the repository worktree is restored to the exact phase-entry tracked state plus the exact phase-entry set and contents of untracked non-ignored files.
+- Ignored files and external caches are not treated as part of the required snapshot unless the implementation already manages a dispatch-owned output path explicitly.
+- Retry cleanup is path-safe: use literal paths, repo-relative manifests, and git commands scoped to the repository root; do not delete outside the repository.
+- Existing queue publishing, GitHub issue comments, label behavior, failure taxonomy, JSONL schemas, verification gates, Rust/Cargo behavior, docs, CI, scheduler behavior, and task-selection brief content are unchanged.
+- Loop output contains distinguishable text for retryable mutation attempt failure, successful restore before retry, successful retry completion, and retry exhaustion.
+- The canonical verification gate passes after the implementation.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not implement broad task retries, queue retries, or GitHub issue retries.
+- Do not retry semantic dispatch outcomes: `EXEC_STATUS: blocked`, `EXEC_STATUS: failed`, failed verification gates, Codex control `needs_changes`, Codex control `block`, Claude plan-gate `needs_changes`, or Claude plan-gate `block`.
+- Do not change `MaxCorrectionRounds`, `MaxPlanRevisions`, control review schema, verification script behavior, queue publish policy, queue label policy, or issue-comment content.
+- Do not add a new JSONL trace event, schema, sidecar schema, or machine-readable retry contract.
+- Do not modify task brief/backlog files or auto-selection doctrine.
+- Do not add permanent test files. The focused restore harness must be inline or temporary and must clean up any temporary files it creates outside the allowed dispatch artifacts.
+- Do not commit, stage, push, open a PR, edit GitHub labels, or post GitHub comments.
+- Do not use a restore strategy that can silently drop phase-entry untracked non-ignored files.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each gate in the EXEC packet:
+
+- PowerShell parser validation for `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`, for example parsing the file with `System.Management.Automation.PSParser` and failing if parse errors are returned. Expected exit 0.
+- `git diff --check` from the repository root. Expected exit 0.
+- A focused restore harness that exercises the actual mutation retry path or newly added restore helper in a temporary git workspace. It must prove at minimum that a failed first mutation attempt can dirty a tracked file, delete or modify a tracked file, create an untracked non-ignored file, and then a retry starts from the captured phase-entry state. Expected exit 0.
+- A zero-retry check, either in the focused harness or as a separate command, proving that `MutationRetryCount 0` or the chosen equivalent performs one mutation attempt and does not run restore/retry output. Expected exit 0.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` from the repository root. Expected exit 0.
+- Final `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, with only allowed ISSUE-198 artifacts and the intended `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` change present.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt with `HANDOFF_STATUS: BLOCKED` and `NEXT_ROLE: PLANNER_AI` if any of the following occur:
+
+- The implementation would require editing any file outside the MAY edit list.
+- Exact restoration of tracked changes plus untracked non-ignored files cannot be implemented safely in PowerShell 5.1.
+- The restore approach would require deleting ignored files, external files, user caches, or files outside the repository.
+- A semantic dispatch outcome would need to be reclassified as retryable to make the feature work.
+- The focused restore harness cannot exercise tracked changes and untracked non-ignored files without adding a permanent repository test or script.
+- The canonical verification gate fails for a reason that cannot be fixed within `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`.
+- The change would require modifying queue publish behavior, GitHub issue or label behavior, failure taxonomy, JSONL schema, verification gates, Rust/Cargo files, docs, CI, scheduler behavior, or task brief/backlog content.
+- Any pre-existing worktree state makes it impossible to distinguish allowed ISSUE-198 changes from unrelated user changes.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The existing loop already has same-phase retry around read-only model review phases. This dispatch is narrower: add snapshot-backed retry only where a retry could otherwise leave repository mutations behind. The safest shape is a separate mutation retry wrapper that captures a worktree snapshot at phase entry, restores it before each retry, and delegates final failure back to the existing `Fail` path so terminal wording and exit behavior remain familiar.
+
+The focused restore harness should avoid permanent test files. A practical approach is to create a temporary git repository outside the project tree, run the new helper or a minimal fake-command dispatch path against that temporary workspace, assert status/content before the retry attempt, and clean up after itself. If the harness uses fake `codex` or `claude` commands, keep them in the temporary workspace or system temp directory and do not leave them in this repository.
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+NEXT_ROLE: EXECUTOR_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---
